turns.thought: [OBSERVE] The swarm is stalled with no improvement for 200 iterations. The global best fitness is 1.85e-07, but the improvement is practically zero (delta ~4e-20). Diversity has collapsed to 9.86e-18 (normalized 1.56e-20). The swarm radius is 5.84e-17, velocity norms are near zero (mean 2.17e-17, zero-ratio 1.0), and direction consistency is 0. All particles have essentially the same fitness (std 1.16e-20). No boundary hits, no velocity clipping. The problem is a 10-dimensional sphere (unimodal basin), so the landscape is smooth and convex, not rugged or deceptive. | [DIAGNOSE] This is a classic case of premature convergence on the sphere function. The swarm has converged to a tiny cluster extremely close to the global optimum but not exactly at it. Velocities have dropped to zero due to loss of diversity, causing stagnation. The root cause is that the swarm's exploration dynamics ceased before reaching the exact minimum, likely because the PSO parameters (inertia/cognitive/social) allowed convergence to accelerate too aggressively. Since the function is unimodal, this is not a deceptive trap; simply an early collapse of search momentum. | [STRATEGIZE] The swarm needs position perturbations to reintroduce diversity and restart exploration near the current best, without losing the good solution region. A moderate fraction of particles should be perturbed gently using Gaussian noise, which is better than full resets (too disruptive when the optimum is so close). The target should be random particles to avoid bias. Additionally, the inertia weight should be set to a moderate value (0.9) to sustain velocity after the perturbation.
